createdFor: Generate a video that explains how plants grow.

constraints.style: Clean educational diagram with mechanical cause-and-effect motion

facts.value: A seed begins germination when it has water, oxygen, and suitable warmth.
Roots anchor the plant and absorb water plus dissolved minerals from soil.
The shoot grows upward and leaves unfold to capture light.
Leaves use light energy, carbon dioxide, and water to make sugars and release oxygen.
Water moves upward through xylem, while sugars move from leaves to growing tissues through phloem.
Plant growth happens as cells divide and enlarge at growing tips and other active tissues.
Mature plants can produce flowers, fruits, and seeds that continue the life cycle.

risks: A silent video needs concise on-screen labels, so the visual design must avoid turning into text-heavy slides.
The topic is broad; this version explains the core growth cycle rather than all plant physiology details.
